changes.exact_quote: Recipient may freely use all Confidential Information retained in unaided memory for any purpose.
changes.proposed_redline: Delete the residual knowledge clause unless expressly approved by the client in writing.

changes.exact_quote: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, attorneys, accountants, consultants, financing sources, and other advisers who have a need to know the information for the Purpose.
changes.proposed_redline: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to Representatives only on a need-to-know basis, provided those Representatives are bound by written or professional duties at least as protective as this Agreement.

changes.exact_quote: For purposes of this clause, the following additional term applies: Recipient may use Confidential Information for the Purpose and for internal business planning, product evaluation, and commercial opportunity analysis related to the parties' discussions.
changes.proposed_redline: Recipient may use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.

changes.exact_quote: Recipient will notify Discloser only after completing a compelled disclosure, even though advance notice is legally permitted. Recipient will provide reasonable cooperation so Discloser may seek protective treatment, to the extent legally permitted. Recipient preserves supporting material for a protective-order request for 8 days and updates the disclosure log within 30 days.
changes.proposed_redline: Before any compelled disclosure, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice and reasonable cooperation so Discloser may seek protective treatment.

summary: All eight T2-N response children reviewed against the v4 answer key.
